# Alignment and RL Fine-Tuning {#sec-18-alignment}

::: {.callout-warning}
**Chapter stub.** This chapter is planned but not yet written. The abstract below is its
contract; drafting sources are listed for provenance.
:::

*Where the update lives is not what the update optimizes. This chapter separates
response-masked instruction tuning from preference supervision, then follows a
comparison through Bradley–Terry reward modeling, KL-regularized policy optimization,
PPO in outline, and DPO. CPU mechanism studies will expose scalar-reward assumptions,
the exact finite-response identity behind DPO, and the point where optimizing a proxy
can make its intended target worse. Alignment will be treated as an evaluation
contract tied to a rubric, annotator population, and coverage regime—not a certificate.*

<!-- draft-sources
seeds:
- `LaTeX/Module 11/11.4RL.tex`
- `LaTeX/Module 11/11L_LLM_Alignment.tex`
- `LaTeX/Module 11/11.4S.tex` (model-card and slice-evaluation material only)
transcripts:
- `(slides-only module)`
harvests:
- ch. 17: "where the update lives is not what the update optimizes"
planned-experiments:
- scalar Bradley--Terry consistency and cyclic-preference failure
- exact finite-response KL optimum and DPO identity under stated assumptions
- proxy-pressure audit with gold utility, realized KL, and feedback-coverage repair
licensing-boundary:
- strip private Colab links, third-party images, paper figures, and library listings
- independently derive all executable code and recreate every published figure
-->
